title: Check Point Quantum IoT Protect Device Discovery
spl:
index=security sourcetype="cp_log"
| where match(lower(product),"(?i)iot.?protect|iot.?discovery")
| stats dc(device_id) as devices values(device_type) as types by policy_name, action
| sort -devices
cimSpl: | tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Network_Traffic.All_Traffic by All_Traffic.action | sort - count
description: IoT Protect uses network fingerprinting to automatically discover and classify IoT/OT devices on the network and apply micro-segmentation policies. Tracking discovered device types and policy assignments validates that IoT assets are protected and not bypassing security.
value: IoT Protect uses network fingerprinting to automatically discover and classify IoT/OT devices on the network and apply micro-segmentation policies. Tracking discovered device types and policy assignments validates that IoT assets are protected and not bypassing security.
implementation: Enable IoT Protect on Quantum gateways. Monitor discovered device counts and types. Alert on new unclassified devices. Validate that IoT-specific policies are being enforced. Report on IoT device inventory growth for asset management.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: `Splunk_TA_checkpoint`.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `sourcetype=cp_log` (IoT Protect discovery/policy events).
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
Enable IoT Protect on Quantum gateways. Monitor discovered device counts and types. Alert on new unclassified devices. Validate that IoT-specific policies are being enforced. Report on IoT device inventory growth for asset management.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=security sourcetype="cp_log"
| where match(lower(product),"(?i)iot.?protect|iot.?discovery")
| stats dc(device_id) as devices values(device_type) as types by policy_name, action
| sort -devices
```

Understanding this SPL

**Check Point Quantum IoT Protect Device Discovery** — IoT Protect uses network fingerprinting to automatically discover and classify IoT/OT devices on the network and apply micro-segmentation policies. Tracking discovered device types and policy assignments validates that IoT assets are protected and not bypassing security.

Documented **Data sources**: `sourcetype=cp_log` (IoT Protect discovery/policy events). **App/TA** (typical add-on context): `Splunk_TA_checkpoint`. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: security; **sourcetype**: cp_log. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Detection type** for this use case: Anomaly — interpret thresholds and fields in that context.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=security, sourcetype="cp_log". Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• Filters the current rows with `where match(lower(product),"(?i)iot.?protect|iot.?discovery")` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by policy_name, action** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions (useful for per-host, per-user, or per-entity comparisons for this use case).
• Orders rows with `sort` — combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.

Optional CIM / accelerated variant (same use case, normalized fields via Common Information Model):

```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Network_Traffic.All_Traffic by All_Traffic.action | sort - count
```

Understanding this CIM / accelerated SPL

**Check Point Quantum IoT Protect Device Discovery** — IoT Protect uses network fingerprinting to automatically discover and classify IoT/OT devices on the network and apply micro-segmentation policies. Tracking discovered device types and policy assignments validates that IoT assets are protected and not bypassing security.

Documented **Data sources**: `sourcetype=cp_log` (IoT Protect discovery/policy events). **App/TA** (typical add-on context): `Splunk_TA_checkpoint`. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

This **CIM or accelerated** block uses normalized field names and/or `tstats` over data models. Enable **acceleration** on the referenced models (and correct CIM knowledge objects) or the search may return nothing.

**Detection type** for this use case: Anomaly — interpret thresholds and fields in that context.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Uses `tstats` against accelerated summaries for data model `Network_Traffic.All_Traffic` — enable acceleration for that model.
• Orders rows with `sort` — combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.

Enable Data Model Acceleration (and metric indexes for `mstats`) for the models or datasets referenced above; otherwise `tstats`/`mstats` may return no results from summaries.


Step 3 — Validate
Confirm that events are present in the index and that the search returns expected results. Compare with known good/bad scenarios if applicable. Verify field extractions and index permissions.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table (discovered devices), Pie chart (device types), Bar chart (policies applied), Line chart (discovery trend).
visualization: Table (discovered devices), Pie chart (device types), Bar chart (policies applied), Line chart (discovery trend).
knownFalsePositives: Seasonal traffic, marketing campaigns, backups or project rollouts can shift the baseline — tune per-entity baselines and widen the training window if the signal becomes noisy.
